message:
feat(recovery,bd-yfdb6): per-path recovery fence + post-checkpoint checksum verification

Harden the two-process crash-recovery path so a SIGKILL mid-write
cannot lose committed rows and cannot race a concurrent opener through
hot-journal / WAL replay.

Recovery fence (pager.rs + recovery_fence.rs)
- Introduce a static RECOVERY_FENCES registry keyed by canonical DB
  path so every SimplePager::open_with_cx* call for the same file
  contends on the same RecoveryFence.
- Connection open now acquires the fence around the hot-journal probe
  and WAL recovery, with bounded backoff (100ms x 10 = 1s) before
  surfacing FrankenError::BusyRecovery for a clean caller retry.
- In-memory databases get a fresh per-connection fence to preserve
  existing isolation semantics.
- Minor whitespace/format cleanups in recovery_fence.rs.

Post-checkpoint checksum verification (checkpoint_executor.rs)
- CheckpointTarget grows an optional read_page_if_supported() hook so
  real VFS-backed targets can read back the page that was just
  staged. Default impl returns None, so tiny in-memory targets are
  unaffected.
- The truncate path accumulates ExpectedPageChecksum for each frame
  whose page data carries a trailer checksum, and verifies the
  on-disk state against the expected checksums before the WAL is
  discarded. If verification would be silent (trailer too small in
  tiny-page tests), the capture is skipped — additive insurance, not
  a hard invariant.

Test
- recovery_data_loss.rs: mostly rustfmt (line length / arg
  wrapping). The two-process SIGKILL harness itself is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
